lesson.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

videos.title: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology

vocabularies.definition: The subjects that are taught in a school, college, etc., or the things that are studied in a particular subject.
examples: The school has a new curriculum for science.
The curriculum includes history, geography, and mathematics.
vocabularies.contextSentence: this is a 40 topic curriculum.

vocabularies.definition: A period of time that is very successful or enjoyable.
examples: The company is experiencing a golden period of growth.
The 1920s were a golden period for jazz music.
vocabularies.contextSentence: But for me right now, this is a real golden period.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is enthusiastic about new technology.
examples: He is a technophile and always buys the latest gadgets.
Technophiles are eager to embrace new technological advancements.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The first group are called technophiles.

vocabularies.definition: A person who dislikes or is afraid of new technology.
examples: My grandmother is a technophobe and struggles with computers.
Technophobes often avoid using modern technology.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The second group are called technophobes.

vocabularies.definition: A small, useful machine or device.
examples: I have a lot of gadgets in my kitchen.
This new gadget can translate any language.
vocabularies.contextSentence: What gadgets do you use on a daily basis?

vocabularies.definition: Without any delay; instantly.
examples: I knew immediately that something was wrong.
The fire alarm went off immediately.
vocabularies.contextSentence: The ones that come to my mind immediately are my phone, my laptop,

vocabularies.definition: To improve something or make it more modern.
examples: I need to upgrade my computer.
The company upgraded its software.
vocabularies.contextSentence: but last year I decided to upgrade to kind of a top of the range.

vocabularies.definition: The process or art of making videos.
examples: He is studying videography at university.
Her passion is videography and creating short films.
vocabularies.contextSentence: because as I said, I do like videography, particularly,

vocabularies.definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.
examples: Many traditional farming methods have become obsolete.
This technology is now obsolete.
vocabularies.contextSentence: A lot of other technology became obsolete.

vocabularies.definition: A new idea, method, or product that completely changes the way that people do something.
examples: The invention of the internet was a game changer.
This new software is a game changer for our business.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I think the the biggest technological development that comes to my mind is the phone, the smart phone, because it was a real game changer.

vocabularies.definition: The state of depending on or trusting in something or someone.
examples: Our reliance on fossil fuels is causing environmental problems.
There is an increasing reliance on technology in education.
vocabularies.contextSentence: Another one that comes to my mind is our reliance on maps

vocabularies.definition: A thing that prevents someone from giving their attention to something else.
examples: The noise was a distraction during the exam.
He found it hard to work with so many distractions.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I find that phones are a distraction.

grammars.title: Use of 'Whether' to introduce alternatives
grammars.explanation: Used to introduce two or more possibilities or choices.
examples: I don't know whether I should go or stay.
Whether you like it or not, you have to do it.
grammars.contextSentence: Whether it's work, I'm using my laptop, my phone

grammars.title: Present Perfect Simple
grammars.explanation: Used to describe an action that started in the past and continues to the present, or an action completed in the recent past.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her homework.
grammars.contextSentence: I've always had kind of okay phones, but last year I decided to upgrade

grammars.title: Past Perfect Simple
grammars.explanation: Used to describe an action that was completed before another action in the past.
examples: I had finished the book before the movie came out.
She had already eaten when I arrived.
grammars.contextSentence: I've had several phones, and I've liked some of them, I've always used Samsung.

grammars.title: Passive Voice
grammars.explanation: Used when the action is more important than the actor.
examples: The book was written by him.
The house is being built.
grammars.contextSentence: So perhaps people's sense of direction, finding our way, that's getting worse nowadays.

grammars.title: Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
grammars.explanation: Used to talk about possible situations and their consequences in the future.
examples: If it rains, I will stay home.
If she studies hard, she will pass the exam.
grammars.structure: If + present simple, will + base form
grammars.contextSentence: if there's something you care about and you're really interested in, use it.

flashcard_set.title: Từ vựng: IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology
flashcard_set.description: Bộ flashcard từ video 'IELTS English Podcast - Speaking Topic: Technology'

cards.backContent:
Nghĩa: chương trình học

Phát âm: /kəˈrɪkjələm/

Ví dụ: The school has a new curriculum for science.

Nghĩa: giai đoạn vàng

Phát âm: /ˈɡəʊldən ˈpɪəriəd/

Ví dụ: The company is experiencing a golden period of growth.

Nghĩa: người yêu công nghệ

Phát âm: /ˈtɛknəfaɪl/

Ví dụ: He is a technophile and always buys the latest gadgets.

Nghĩa: người sợ công nghệ

Phát âm: /ˈtɛknəfəʊb/

Ví dụ: My grandmother is a technophobe and struggles with computers.

Nghĩa: tiện ích, đồ dùng

Phát âm: /ˈɡædʒɪt/

Ví dụ: I have a lot of gadgets in my kitchen.

Đồng nghĩa: contraption, contrivance, doohickey, gizmo

Nghĩa: ngay lập tức

Phát âm: /ɪˈmiːdiətli/

Ví dụ: I knew immediately that something was wrong.

Đồng nghĩa: forthwith, now, tout de suite, after

Nghĩa: nâng cấp

Phát âm: /ʌpˈɡreɪd/

Ví dụ: I need to upgrade my computer.

Nghĩa: nghệ thuật quay phim

Phát âm: /ˌvɪdiˈɒɡrəfi/

Ví dụ: He is studying videography at university.

Nghĩa: lỗi thời

Phát âm: /ˈɒbsəliːt/

Ví dụ: Many traditional farming methods have become obsolete.

Đồng nghĩa: abortive, obscure, rudimental, antiquated

Nghĩa: thay đổi cuộc chơi

Phát âm: /ˈɡeɪm ˌtʃeɪndʒər/

Ví dụ: The invention of the internet was a game changer.

Nghĩa: sự phụ thuộc vào

Phát âm: /rɪˈlaɪəns ɒn/

Ví dụ: Our reliance on fossil fuels is causing environmental problems.

Nghĩa: sự xao nhãng

Phát âm: /dɪˈstrækʃən/

Ví dụ: The noise was a distraction during the exam.

lesson.title: Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writing Task 2 (Agree or Disagree)

videos.title: Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writing Task 2 (Agree or Disagree)

vocabularies.definition: difficult in an interesting or enjoyable way
examples: The exam was challenging but rewarding.
It's a challenging task, but I'm confident I can do it.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I've selected quite a challenging topic that was used in real exams

vocabularies.definition: used to ask or talk about how much something is true or how great something is
examples: To what extent do you agree with this statement?
To what extent is this problem serious?
vocabularies.contextSentence: to what extent do you agree or disagree you know the rest right write it

vocabularies.definition: to keep something at the same level or standard
examples: The company is struggling to maintain its market share.
It's important to maintain a healthy lifestyle.
vocabularies.contextSentence: your ability to maintain a good point of view

vocabularies.definition: urgent or needing to be dealt with immediately
examples: There are pressing issues that need to be addressed.
The government is facing pressing economic problems.
vocabularies.contextSentence: that even though there are pressing problems on Earth this is still money well spent

vocabularies.definition: to change the use of something, especially money or resources
examples: The company is diverting resources to a new project.
The government is diverting funds from education to defense.
vocabularies.contextSentence: I firmly believe that diverting financial resources from space exploration would be a mistake

vocabularies.definition: positive effects or advantages for all people
examples: Technological advancements have brought numerous benefits to humanity.
The discovery of penicillin was a major benefit to humanity.
vocabularies.contextSentence: these projects have brought and will continue to bring great benefits to humanity

vocabularies.definition: expressing the meaning of something using different words, especially to achieve greater clarity
examples: The student was able to paraphrase the complex text.
Paraphrasing is an important skill for academic writing.
vocabularies.contextSentence: In the first notice the paraphrasing spend too much money on Space research becomes the millions

vocabularies.definition: serious problems that need immediate attention
examples: Climate change is one of the most urgent issues facing the world today.
The government must address the urgent issues of poverty and inequality.
vocabularies.contextSentence: would be better invested in addressing urgent issues on this planet the

vocabularies.definition: made an action or process easy or easier
examples: The new software facilitated the work of the team.
The new road facilitated trade between the two countries.
vocabularies.contextSentence: have not only facilitated scientific advances in areas such as weather monitoring and navigation

vocabularies.definition: very great or intense
examples: The experience had a profound effect on her.
The book offers a profound insight into human nature.
vocabularies.contextSentence: we have witnessed profound changes in the way we connect with each other using the internet to

vocabularies.definition: connected with each other
examples: The world is becoming increasingly interconnected.
The internet has made the world more interconnected.
vocabularies.contextSentence: satellite technology has interconnected the world shaping the way we live today

vocabularies.definition: a useful result of an activity designed to produce something else
examples: The company's research led to several spin-offs.
Space exploration has resulted in many technological spin-offs.
vocabularies.contextSentence: these spinoffs cover a wide range from comfortable memory phone mattresses to Tiny cameras in Mobile

vocabularies.definition: the newest and most advanced
examples: The company uses cutting-edge technology.
They are developing cutting-edge medical treatments.
vocabularies.contextSentence: We have some topic specific vocabulary which is great to have cuttingedge Technologies to mean

vocabularies.definition: the action of spending funds
examples: The government's expenditure on education has increased.
The company needs to reduce its expenditure.
vocabularies.contextSentence: there is no doubt that the expenditure on Space research is enormous this

grammars.title: Complex Sentences with 'Although'
grammars.explanation: Using 'although' (or 'though') to introduce a concessive clause, showing contrast.
examples: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
Though she was tired, she kept working.
grammars.contextSentence: Although there are undoubtedly many problems on Earth that need tackling I firmly believe that diverting financial resources from space

grammars.title: Paraphrasing with Passive Voice
grammars.explanation: Changing active voice to passive voice to rephrase the question.
examples: The government spends too much money -> The millions spent annually on government funded space projects...
They are building a new bridge -> A new bridge is being built.
grammars.contextSentence: spend too much money on Space research becomes the millions spent on government funded space projects would be better invested

grammars.title: Using Commas with Dependent Clauses
grammars.explanation: Correct punctuation when a dependent clause starts a sentence.
examples: Because it was raining, we stayed inside.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
grammars.contextSentence: In this complex sentence very important we need a comma examiners do check if you punctuate correctly so we have our

grammars.title: Topic Sentences
grammars.explanation: Starting a paragraph with a sentence that introduces the main idea.
examples: The development of satellite technology has greatly improved our lives.
There are several benefits to the use of renewable energy.
grammars.structure: A clear statement of the paragraph's focus.
grammars.contextSentence: we're going to start with the topic sentence and then give some examples and make any relevant comments

grammars.title: Not only...but also...
grammars.explanation: A correlative conjunction used to emphasize two related points.
examples: Not only did he pass the exam, but he also got a scholarship.
Satellites have not only helped scientists make progress, but have also changed our daily lives.
grammars.structure: Not only + auxiliary verb + subject + verb, but also + verb...
grammars.contextSentence: satellites orbiting the Earth have not only facilitated scientific advances but have also transformed our daily lives

grammars.title: Using Present Participles for Sentence Variety
grammars.explanation: Using -ing forms to combine sentences and add detail.
examples: She walked to the store, buying some milk.
He studied hard, passing the exam.
grammars.contextSentence: because of satellites we have seen big changes in how we connect with each other comma using the internet

grammars.title: Present Perfect Tense
grammars.explanation: Using the present perfect to show the ongoing impact of past events.
examples: I have lived here for five years.
She has finished her work.
grammars.contextSentence: the verb tense is mainly the present perfect to show how developments which began some time ago continue to have effect on

grammars.title: Referencing
grammars.explanation: Using pronouns and other words to refer back to previously mentioned ideas.
examples: These examples illustrate the point.
It is important to remember the main idea.
grammars.structure: Using words like 'these', 'this', 'it', etc. to connect ideas.
grammars.contextSentence: be careful to use referencing in your essay because examiners check if you do now

flashcard_set.title: Từ vựng: Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writi
flashcard_set.description: Bộ flashcard từ video 'Nail Your Opinion Essay in 17 Minutes: IELTS Writing Task 2 (Agree or Disagree)'

cards.backContent:
Nghĩa: thử thách

Phát âm: /ˈtʃælɪndʒɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The exam was challenging but rewarding.

Đồng nghĩa: becall, call out

Nghĩa: ở mức độ nào

Phát âm: /tuː wɒt ɪkˈstɛnt/

Ví dụ: To what extent do you agree with this statement?

Nghĩa: duy trì

Phát âm: /meɪnˈteɪn/

Ví dụ: The company is struggling to maintain its market share.

Nghĩa: cấp bách

Phát âm: /ˈprɛsɪŋ/

Ví dụ: There are pressing issues that need to be addressed.

Đồng nghĩa: depress, hit, strike, emphasize

Nghĩa: chuyển hướng

Phát âm: /daɪˈvɜːrtɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The company is diverting resources to a new project.

Đồng nghĩa: offlead

Nghĩa: lợi ích cho nhân loại

Phát âm: /ˈbɛnɪfɪts tuː hjuːˈmænɪti/

Ví dụ: Technological advancements have brought numerous benefits to humanity.

Nghĩa: diễn giải

Phát âm: /ˈpærəfreɪzɪŋ/

Ví dụ: The student was able to paraphrase the complex text.

Nghĩa: vấn đề cấp bách

Phát âm: /ˈɜːrdʒənt ˈɪʃuːz/

Ví dụ: Climate change is one of the most urgent issues facing the world today.

Nghĩa: tạo điều kiện

Phát âm: /fəˈsɪlɪteɪtɪd/

Ví dụ: The new software facilitated the work of the team.

Đồng nghĩa: ease

Nghĩa: sâu sắc

Phát âm: /prəˈfaʊnd/

Ví dụ: The experience had a profound effect on her.

Nghĩa: liên kết

Phát âm: /ˌɪntərkəˈnɛktɪd/

Ví dụ: The world is becoming increasingly interconnected.

Đồng nghĩa: anastomose

Nghĩa: sản phẩm phụ

Phát âm: /ˈspɪnˌɒfs/

Ví dụ: The company's research led to several spin-offs.

Đồng nghĩa: derivate, descendant, hive-off

Nghĩa: tối tân

Phát âm: /ˌkʌtɪŋ ˈɛdʒ/

Ví dụ: The company uses cutting-edge technology.

Nghĩa: chi tiêu

Phát âm: /ɪkˈspɛndɪtʃər/

Ví dụ: The government's expenditure on education has increased.

Đồng nghĩa: charge, cost, disbursement, expense
